mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS level transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation support; when rates are near zero, discount-rate pressure is muted and S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in low-rate episodes and the conditional hit rate is below 55% over rolling 3-year windows, reject the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens discounting and liquidity conditions, so equity prices should fall over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of events or if the average event return is not negative in two consecutive tightening subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reading signals weakening household income and profit expectations; once unemployment increases sharply, equities should underperform over the next month as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, retail sales growth is backed by broad labor income growth rather than price inflation, implying stronger near-term demand and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-RSAFS/high-PAYEMS state does not outperform the single-variable states, or if the joint signal fails to produce a positive average 20-day SPX return over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO without a matching rise in inflation indicates stronger real output and calmer supply conditions, which should support equity margins and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and flat-to-lower CPIAUCSL do not show positive 60-day SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the conditional excess return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST after an earlier peak signals demand exhaustion in interest-sensitive sectors and broader cyclical weakness, which should translate into negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences or if the average event return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration phase, a joint rise in UNRATE and contraction in PAYEMS identifies a true income shock rather than a survey noise blip, and the S&P 500 should decline over the next 5 trading days as recession odds reprice immediately.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined UNRATE-up/PAYEMS-down state does not produce negative 5-day returns more than 60% of the time or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from random across rolling 2-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive after a weak prior period, it confirms a recovery in aggregate demand that should lift earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after a negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP inflection points are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean.
